location.office_time_required: 4 days a week in Mountain View office

level_signals: 4+ years of experience in product management
Responsibilities include roadmap ownership, LLM portfolio strategy, and capacity planning

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4+ years of experience in product management at SaaS companies, primarily serving large enterprises with responsibilities spanning AI/ML Ops and external-facing platform capabilities.
You have a strong technical background and experience with both UX and API driven products and 3rd party product partnerships.
You are an excellent written and verbal communicator.
You have a proven track record of taking ownership, taking initiative, and delivering results.
You collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners.
You have a learning and growth mindset.
You are mission-first and understand that your success is measured by your product and team's success.
You are an early adopter in building with or adopting AI for your own product craft and you are excited about helping customers accelerate their own AI adoption journey.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Having built or worked with LLMs for at-scale products is a major plus.

scope.surface: Glean Model Hub — LLM portfolio management, inference/model provider relationships, customer LLM experimentation, LLM usage/cost/capacity planning
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer upgrade adoption rate for latest LLMs
core_responsibilities: Evaluate LLM models and define roadmap for growing Glean's LLM portfolio
Manage relationships with inference and model providers
Determine product strategy for allowing customers to experiment with different LLMs
Own projections of LLM usage, cost, and capacity planning
Drive customer enablement and upgrade adoption journey for latest LLMs
Spending a lot of time with customers to deeply understand their knowledge needs
Coordinating cross-functionally with leadership, go-to-market teams, and other key stakeholders
Building processes that will scale as team and company go through rapid growth

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as eligible for certain roles

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($160K-$240K base)
Clear product ownership area (Glean Model Hub) with well-defined scope
High-growth company with strong market recognition (Fast Company Top 10, Forbes AI 50, etc.)
Comprehensive benefits including home office stipend, education and wellness stipends, daily lunches
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires 4 days/week in Mountain View office — eliminates remote and most hybrid candidates
Role title is 'AI Quality' but JD body describes a 'PM for Glean Model Hub' — title and body describe different or overlapping scopes, potential internal inconsistency
Very wide comp band ($80K spread) may indicate significant uncertainty about level

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
ambiguities: Role title is 'Product Manager, AI Quality' but the JD body describes the role as 'PM for Glean Model Hub' with no explicit mention of 'AI Quality' in responsibilities. These may be the same role or there may be a mismatch between the posted title and the actual scope.
Equity is mentioned as available for 'certain roles' without confirming this role qualifies — treated as equity_mentioned: true with caveat.
Variable compensation mentioned but it is unclear if this role specifically qualifies.
